history.what_worked: Per-skill learnings/<skill>.json files keep memory scoped and loadable on demand, matching the file-based memory pattern that 2026 surveys (Mem0 State of AI Agent Memory, MachineLearningMastery, Spring AI AutoMemoryTools) report outperforms vector retrieval for autonomous agents.
history.what_didnt: No upper bound on history[] array size and no curation step. Published guidance (dev.to/kfuras 2026) recommends a hard cap around 100 lines per learnings file with a weekly review that promotes recurring observations into rules and archives the rest. Without that, learnings/ files will bloat over months and dilute signal — the agent is supposed to read the whole file each session.
history.rule_change: When .agent/learnings/<skill>.json grows past ~100 lines or ~20 history entries, run a curation pass: deduplicate near-identical entries, promote recurring patterns into a single canonical rule, and move stale entries to .agent/learnings/archive/<skill>-<date>.json. Schedule this as a periodic agent task rather than letting files grow unbounded.
